metadata.name: Information Delivery Process
metadata.description: Delivery-phase information management workflow defined in ISO 19650-2:2018, showing the eight activities and their relationship to project stages and information exchange milestones.
metadata.author: ISO19650 Knowledge Maps

nodes.label: Assessment of Need
nodes.description: Initial stage where the appointing party assesses project or asset needs and defines information requirements, standard, and CDE.

nodes.label: Invitation to Tender
nodes.description: Stage where information requirements (EIR) are issued to potential suppliers along with reference information.

nodes.label: Tender Response
nodes.description: Suppliers respond with BIM execution plan, capability and capacity assessments, mobilization plan, and risk register.

nodes.label: Appointment
nodes.description: The appointing party appoints the delivery team, confirming BIM execution plan and establishing delivery plans.

nodes.label: Mobilization
nodes.description: Teams prepare systems, resources, test the CDE, and confirm information production methods and procedures.

nodes.label: Collaborative Production of Information
nodes.description: Delivery teams produce and coordinate information within the CDE, following quality assurance and review processes.

nodes.label: Information Model Delivery
nodes.description: Delivery of the project information model at defined exchange milestones, with review and authorization by lead appointed party and acceptance by appointing party.

nodes.label: Project Closeout
nodes.description: Completion of delivery, archiving of project information model, and transition of information to asset management (AIM).

nodes.label: Project Stage
nodes.description: A defined phase of the project (e.g., design, construction) at the end of which key decisions are made.

nodes.label: Key Decision Point
nodes.description: Point in time during the project when a decision crucial to the direction or viability of the asset is made.

nodes.label: Information Exchange Milestone
nodes.description: Defined milestone, typically at the end of a project stage, where verified information is exchanged to support decision-making.

nodes.label: Exchange Information Requirements (EIR)
nodes.description: Information requirements defined for an appointment, specifying what, when, and how information is to be delivered.

nodes.label: BIM Execution Plan
nodes.description: Plan explaining how the information management aspects of the appointment will be carried out by the delivery team.

nodes.label: Master Information Delivery Plan (MIDP)
nodes.description: Plan incorporating all task information delivery plans, listing information containers and delivery dates.

nodes.label: Information Model
nodes.description: Structured collection of information containers produced during project delivery.

nodes.label: Project Information Model (PIM)
nodes.description: Information model relating to the delivery phase, which at closeout contributes to the AIM.

nodes.label: Asset Information Model (AIM)
nodes.description: Information model used during asset operation, receiving information from the PIM at project closeout.

nodes.label: Common Data Environment
nodes.description: Shared information environment used to manage and exchange information throughout the delivery process.

nodes.label: Lead Appointed Party
nodes.description: Party responsible for coordinating the delivery team and authorizing information before delivery.

nodes.label: Appointing Party
nodes.description: The party receiving information and responsible for defining requirements and accepting information exchanges.

evidence.quote: The appointing party assesses the need and defines information requirements prior to inviting tenders.
Potential suppliers respond to the invitation to tender with proposals for information delivery.
Following evaluation of tender responses, the appointing party appoints the selected suppliers.
Following appointment, teams mobilize to prepare for information production.
Mobilization establishes the systems and resources required for collaborative production of information.
Information is produced collaboratively in preparation for information exchange milestones.
Information exchanges occur at defined milestones where information models are delivered.
Information is delivered at project closeout to support asset management.
Key decision points generally align with project stages.
Information is required to support key decisions during the project.
Exchange information requirements specify the information to be delivered at information exchanges.
The appointing party nominates individuals to undertake the information management function.
The appointing party establishes their exchange information requirements.
The prospective lead appointed party establishes the delivery team's BIM execution plan.
The lead appointed party aggregates the TIDPs to establish the master information delivery plan.
Information containers collectively form the project information model.
The project information model supports project delivery.
Information developed during project delivery contributes to the asset information model.
Information is produced and managed within the common data environment during collaborative production.
The common data environment facilitates information exchange and delivery.
